utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hi , natalie . how are you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: good . how are you ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: it's good to see you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: good to see you , too .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i know my nurse has already told you about dax . is it okay if i tell dax a little bit about you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: go for it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . so , natalie is a 39-year-old female , here today for a check-up for an abnormal lab . she's got a history of high blood pressure , diabetes , and acl repair .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , tell me what's going on .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: so , uh , i got some re- routine blood work done last week and , uh , they said that my blood sugars were , were high , so that i should come in , um , and talk to you and get some more work done and , and , uh , see why that is .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . well , were you having any symptoms at all or just , we had some blood work done routinely ? uh , any chest pain , shortness of breath ? any nausea , vomiting , or diarrhea ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , no symptoms . uh , so i was a little shocked when i came back high and , and elevated , so hopefully we can figure out what's going on .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: we've been , kind of , checking on this for a while and you've been on metformin . we actually increased your dose last time-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yup .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so right now you're on 100 , 1,000 milligrams a day , correct ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: correct . yup .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . no , uh , no difficulty urinating or burning when you urinate at all ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: nope .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . um , hey , dragon , show me the hemoglobin a1c . so in reviewing your , that's the lab that we checked after when i saw before i walked in . it says 8.1 , which is elevated , so i think we need to do a maybe a better job with adjusting your medication .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: how are you doing with your diet for your diabetes ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: my diet's been really great lately . i've been avoiding sugars , um , been really watching , uh , my weight and power walking every day , so i was a little surprised when the , the blood work came back that i- i had , um , an elevated , uh , test .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and , uh , have you had any , uh , any other , are you checking your blood sugars in the morning or your fasting blood sugars ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , i am .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . how are you doing with your high blood pressure ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: that's been good and stable . um , i've been stable on the medication , been taking that every day . um , and i have a b- a cuff at home to , to measure it myself and that , everything's been normal .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , great . and how's your knee doing that's the other issue we've been , kind of , following ? you had some surgery done for that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah . about five years ago , i had , um , an acl repair on my right knee and everything's been going well so far . i have n't had any , uh , issues with mobility . um , it's been great .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and you're pretty physically active and doing exercises ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah . i've been power walking about , at least 30 minutes every day .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . great . well , let's review , uh , some of your vital signs . hey , dragon , show me the vitals . all right . blood pressure looks good right now . your oxygen level's great . uh , your temperature's good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so i know you checked in with the review of systems with my assistant before coming in . uh , anything else bothering you today except for the abnormal blood work ? no chest pain ? no shortness of breath ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , nothing like that ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: no headaches ? no belly pain ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: good . great . well , let me examine you , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright . just gon na listen to your heart here .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: no belly pain ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: nope .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so on exam , uh , your hent or your neck exam is fine . your , uh , your eyes look fine . your lungs are clear . i do hear a systolic ejection murmur , about three over six . your belly's nice and soft . no swelling in your legs or extremities right now .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so let's talk about some of the issues today . all right . for the first issue , for the diabetes , since your hemoglobin a1c is is elevated , uh , and you're already on 1,000 milligrams of metformin , i think we should add , uh , jardiance , uh , 10 milligrams a day . uh , so that's a new medication . we'll start that . i do want you to , uh follow your diet pretty , uh , aggressively . i wan na see if we can get that hemoglobin a1c down the next time .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'm also gon na add some labs today to see what that , to see a lipid panel and some other blood work . hey , dragon , order a cbc , cmp , and a lipid panel . and i'll go ahead and prescribe the jardiance , 10 milligrams a day . hey , dragon , prescribe , uh , order jardiance , 10 milligrams a day .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . for the high blood pressure , i think you're doing great . you're on lisinopril for that , so i think that we should continue that . uh , we'll re-check it again next time and you are monitoring that at home , correct ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , i am .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , great . and for the knee , i think you're doing great with that . you know , continue with your physical exercise , with your physical therapy , uh , uh , with your normal exercise regimen . uh , if you have any issues with that , certainly give us a call .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . any other questions ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , that's it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . hey , dragon , finalize the note .

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 39-year-old female with a history of hypertension, diabetes, and right ACL repair.
Abnormal labs
The patient had routine blood work showing high blood sugar levels.
The doctor asks if the patient has symptoms including chest pain, shortness of breath, nausea, vomiting, or diarrhea.
The patient denies having any symptoms.
The patient is taking metformin 1000 mg daily.
The doctor asks if the patient has difficulty urinating or burning during urination.
The patient denies urinary difficulty or burning.
The patient's hemoglobin A1c is 8.1, which is elevated.
The doctor asks how the patient is doing with her diabetes diet.
The patient reports avoiding sugars, watching her weight, and power walking daily.
The doctor asks if the patient is checking her fasting blood sugars in the morning.
The patient confirms she is checking her blood sugars every morning.
Hypertension
The patient reports stable blood pressure on lisinopril and monitors it at home.
Knee
The patient had an ACL repair on her right knee five years ago and has no mobility issues.
The doctor asks if the patient is physically active.
The patient power walks for 30 minutes every day.
Vital signs including blood pressure, oxygen level, and temperature are normal.
The doctor asks if the patient has any other concerns, chest pain, or shortness of breath.
The patient denies other concerns, chest pain, or shortness of breath.
The doctor asks if the patient has headaches or abdominal pain.
The patient denies headaches and abdominal pain.
The doctor declares the intent to perform a physical examination.
The doctor asks if the patient has abdominal pain.
The patient denies abdominal pain.
Physical exam findings: normal head/neck/eyes, clear lungs, 3/6 systolic ejection murmur, soft abdomen, no lower extremity edema.
The doctor prescribes Jardiance 10 mg daily for diabetes.
The doctor encourages the patient to follow dietary modifications closely.
The doctor orders a CBC, CMP, and lipid panel.
The doctor advises continuing lisinopril for hypertension.
The doctor confirms the patient is monitoring blood pressure at home.
The doctor advises continuing the current exercise regimen for the knee.
The doctor asks if the patient has any other questions.

case_description: abnormal labs; hypertension;type 2 diabetes;hx right acl repair
